name: German Stollen
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium bowl, combine raisins, cherries, currants, citron or citrus peel, and rum. Set aside.
2. In a large mixing bowl, combine 1-1/2 cups of the flour and yeast. Heat milk, butter, sugar, and salt over low heat, stirring constantly until warm (120°F to 130°F).
3. Add the heated mixture to the dry ingredients along with eggs and fruit peels. Beat at low speed of an electric mixer for 1/2 minute, scraping sides of the bowl.
4. Beat on high speed for 3 minutes. Stir in the fruit-rum mixture, almonds, and enough of the remaining flour to make a soft dough. Turn out onto a lightly floured surface and knead with enough of the remaining flour to make a moderately soft dough that is smooth and elastic (5 to 6 minutes).
5. Shape into a ball. Place in a greased bowl, turning once. Cover. Let rise until double (1 hour).
6. Punch down the dough. Divide into two equal pieces. Cover and let rest for 10 minutes.
7. On a lightly floured surface, roll each half to a 10x8-inch oval. Fold lengthwise in half so the top half overlaps the bottom half by about 1/2 inch. Press folded edge firmly.
8. Place the loaves about 4 inches apart on a greased baking sheet. Cover and let rise until double (45 minutes).
9. Bake in a 375°F oven for 15 to 20 minutes or until golden brown. Cool.
Serve sprinkled with powdered sugar or spread with confectioners' glaze and decorate with candied cherries. Makes 2 loaves.
Confectioners' Glaze:
Mix 1 cup sifted powdered sugar and 1 tablespoon milk. Add more milk, 1/2 teaspoon at a time, until spreading consistency.
